evals.name: initiate-login-uri-lookup
evals.prompt: I'm configuring an OIDC-style integration and saw the term `initiate_login_uri` mentioned. I'm using WorkOS AuthKit. What exactly is this and where do I actually configure it? Give me the canonical docs URL so I can read more. Keep the answer tight.

assertions.text: Mentions Sign-in endpoint as the WorkOS-side term for this concept
Points to Dashboard → Redirects as the config location
Cites an actual workos.com/docs URL (not llms.txt)
Does NOT invent a URL like /docs/initiate-login-uri
Does NOT use llms.txt as the primary source

evals.name: sign-in-endpoint-lookup
evals.prompt: I'm staring at the WorkOS Dashboard and I see a field called 'Sign-in endpoint' in the Redirects section. What IS this exactly — why does WorkOS need it if I'm already passing a Redirect URI? Link me to the canonical docs page.

assertions.text: Distinguishes Sign-in endpoint from Redirect URI
Explains the bookmark / password-reset use case

evals.name: cookie-password-lookup
evals.prompt: My WorkOS AuthKit app is throwing an error about `WORKOS_COOKIE_PASSWORD` being invalid. What is this env var, what are the requirements (length, format), how do I generate a valid one, and where are the official docs?

assertions.text: Shows how to generate one with openssl

evals.name: org-vs-connection-id
evals.prompt: For my SSO integration I see both Organization ID and Connection ID as options when generating an authorization URL. What's the actual difference, and which one should I pass to getAuthorizationUrl in typical multi-tenant B2B setup? Docs link please.

assertions.text: Recommends Organization ID for multi-tenant B2B
Explains what Connection ID is and when to use it
Explains why Organization ID is preferred (connection routing)
